title: Maths / Science Teacher
description: Teach Mathematics, Physical Sciences, or Life Sciences at secondary school. One of the most impactful and in-demand roles in SA's education system, with a clear permanent-post pathway.

employability.demand: Very high — acute shortage of qualified Maths and Science teachers nationally.
employability.outlook: Strong; NSFAS bursaries incentivise the pathway; public posts are stable.
pathways: BEd (4 years) with Mathematics / Science specialism
DBE, SGBs — permanent public school posts

title: Foundation / Intermediate Phase Teacher
description: Teach Grades R–6, laying the foundations for literacy, numeracy, and life skills. Work with young learners in public, independent, and ECD settings.

employability.demand: High — shortage of qualified Foundation Phase teachers, especially in rural areas.
employability.outlook: Stable; early childhood development policy expanding the sector.
pathways: BEd Foundation Phase / Intermediate Phase (4 years)

title: Psychologist
description: Assess and treat mental health conditions — depression, anxiety, trauma, and behavioural disorders. Work in clinical, educational, industrial, and community settings.

employability.demand: High — SA has fewer than 5,000 registered psychologists for 62 million people.
employability.outlook: Strong; mental health awareness driving demand in corporate and educational sectors.
pathways: Hons + Postgrad Diploma / Masters in Psychology (2–3 years)

title: Social Worker
description: Support individuals, families, and communities facing poverty, abuse, substance dependence, and social exclusion. Carry statutory duties in child protection, welfare, and mental health.

employability.demand: High — DSD is chronically understaffed; NGO and community sector demand is large.
employability.outlook: Steady; government salary restructuring improving retention.
pathways: DSD, children's homes, hospitals, schools, NGOs

title: Sociologist / Political Scientist
description: Study social behaviour, institutions, and political systems — informing policy, advocacy, research, and public-sector decision-making. Often combined with data skills for evidence-based policy work.

employability.demand: Moderate — government, think tanks, NGOs, and academia are primary employers.
employability.outlook: Stable; research and policy analysis roles growing in public sector.
pathways: Government departments, Parliament, research institutes, NGOs, academia

title: Counsellor / Youth Development Worker
description: Provide guidance and support to individuals navigating personal, career, or substance-use challenges. Work in schools, clinics, community centres, and corporate wellness programmes.

employability.demand: Moderate — EAP and school counselling growing; most posts are in NGO and public sector.
employability.outlook: Stable; mental health awareness increasing demand especially in corporate sector.
pathways: Certificate in Counselling / Youth Work (1 year)
Schools, clinics, rehab centres, EAPs, NGOs

title: Lecturer / Academic Researcher
description: Teach at university level and/or conduct original research in a chosen discipline. Requires postgraduate study and typically a doctoral qualification for permanent academic posts.

employability.demand: Moderate — higher-education sector competes for postdocs; PhD required for most posts.
employability.outlook: Stable; STEM disciplines and scarce-skills fields attract research funding.
pathways: Universities, CSIR, NRF research chairs, private higher education

title: Criminologist / Forensic Practitioner
description: Study crime, criminal behaviour, and the justice system — developing prevention strategies, advising on policy, or working as an expert in forensic settings alongside SAPS and the NPA.

employability.demand: Moderate — SAPS, NPA, prisons, and NGOs are main employers.
employability.outlook: Stable; crime prevention policy and victim support expanding the field.
pathways: SAPS, NPA, DCS, victim support NGOs, private security consulting
